title: [Bug]: `left outer join` some `view` may produce unexpected result.
description: `left outer join` some `view` produces unexpected result.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO t_test (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test1', 10.50), (2, 'Test2', NULL), (3, 'Test3', 20.00);
CREATE VIEW v_test AS SELECT id, name, value FROM t_test WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT v1.name, v2.value FROM v_test v1 LEFT JOIN v_test v2 ON v1.id = v2.id WHERE v2.value IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE t_another (ref_id INT, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES t_test(id));
key_bug_features: left outer join on views produces unexpected results
